title: New Proposal Would Allow Administration to Block Grants if They Don’t Support Trump’s Agenda
summary: A new proposal would allow the administration to block grants if they do not satisfy President Trump’s agenda or support what it calls “anti-American” values.

title: Trump Names Bill Pulte as Acting Director of National Intelligence
summary: Bill Pulte, a Trump loyalist who leads the federal housing finance agency, will oversee the U.S. intelligence agencies. He has no known background in intelligence.

title: Trump Signs Executive Order Seeking Oversight of A.I. Models
summary: The order, which signaled a shift from the hands-off approach the White House had previously taken toward A.I., followed debates over how to gain control of A.I. models without disrupting innovation.

title: Polls of California Governor’s Race Show a Clear Top 3
summary: Xavier Becerra, Steve Hilton and Tom Steyer have consistently led the field in California’s nonpartisan primary, where the top two finishers advance.

title: What to Watch in Tuesday’s Primary Elections in Iowa, Montana and Beyond
summary: Democrats have a competitive Senate primary race in Iowa, where the party is hoping to make gains, while a smokejumper is running in a contested House primary in Montana.

title: Only the Right Tests Can Stop This Ebola Outbreak. Congo Has Hardly Any.
summary: A chronic lack of investment in development of better tests has left clinicians blind and allows deadly viruses to spread unchecked.

title: U.S. Ebola Unit Plans in Kenya, Subject of Protests, Suffers New Setback From Court Ruling
summary: The court further delayed the Trump administration’s proposed quarantine unit for Americans exposed to the virus. The plan has sparked angry protests in Kenya.

title: How Three Chess Friends Battled Demons and Saved Two Lives
summary: A homeless chess hustler, a scholar and an aging recluse found hope in a trashed apartment by Central Park.

title: Bombs Raining Down on Ukraine Can’t Hide Signs of Russia’s Faltering War
summary: The war has not been going the Kremlin’s way, with battleground losses and growing casualties. With fiercer strikes, Moscow hopes to gain a better position for negotiations.

title: Russia Launches Deadly Strikes on Kyiv After Threatening Ukraine for a Week
summary: Moscow’s repeated warnings of a major strike, combined with the delay before it happened, seemed intended to inflict a psychological toll on the Ukrainian capital.

title: The Toll of a ‘Missing Scientists’ Conspiracy Theory on the Families Left Behind
summary: The claim that their loved ones’ deaths and disappearances are linked is almost certainly false — but the loss remains real.

title: Obama Center’s Two Sides: A Lovely Park and a Forbidding Tower
summary: In Chicago, the $850 million Obama Presidential Center aims to remake a neighborhood with a 19.3-acre community hub and a brooding 225-foot museum.

title: What Ireland and Germany Can Teach Us About Birthright Citizenship
summary: President Trump has argued that the United States is “stupid” for granting citizenship at birth. Most countries don’t do so, but that can create problems.

title: Carney Says Canada Faces a ‘Crisis of Antisemitism’
summary: Prime Minister Mark Carney’s comments followed calls for action from Jewish organizations and after a government report found that antisemitism had surged in Canada in recent years.

title: ‘La La Land’ Orchestral Performance Saved by Keyboardist in the Audience
summary: After a musician fell ill during a live performance of the score from “La La Land,” the composer Justin Hurwitz asked for a sight reader. A 21-year-old student stepped up.

title: Netanyahu’s Critics Accuse Him of Running Israel as a U.S. ‘Vassal State’
summary: Critics pounced on the Israeli prime minister after President Trump claimed on social media he had persuaded him to call off “a major raid” on Hezbollah in Beirut.

title: In Her Memoir, Jill Biden Is a Watchful Spouse Who Didn’t Always Speak Up About Joe Biden
summary: The former first lady’s new book reflects an insular White House where loyalty was prized and President Joseph R. Biden Jr.’s feelings were prioritized over health concerns.

title: Book Review: ‘View From the East Wing,’ by Jill Biden
summary: Beyond a few pointed digs at her husband’s successor, “View From the East Wing” largely sticks to the head-spinning details of first lady-hood.

title: Trump Megadonor Gave $5.5 Million Estate to Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s Anti-Vaccine Group
summary: A Mellon heir transferred the adjacent Connecticut properties, covering about 300 acres, at no cost to Children’s Health Defense last year.

title: A Stray Dog Became a Cultural Symbol in Brazil. Now, Mexico Is Claiming It.
summary: To Brazilians, the ubiquitous caramel-colored mutts are a national icon. But a Mexican state has adopted them as its own homegrown symbol.

title: Trump Has Failed as Commander in Chief
summary: Trump has failed to unite the country while at war and instead is seeking personal gain.

title: Dear President Ozymandias
summary: Look on his works and despair.

title: Curfew to Be Lifted Near NJ’s Delaney Hall ICE Facility Where Protests Have Raged
summary: The restrictions outside Delaney Hall immigration detention center were imposed after demonstrators who were protesting the conditions at the facility had clashed with the police.
